author: Dr. Linda Wong
department: Family Medicine — Johns Hopkins Community Health Partnership
content:
FOLLOW-UP — 11/12/2019, 11:00 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (Age 16)
Interpreter: Spanish phone

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Routine f/u after iron-deficiency anemia treatment course (initiated 8/2018).

INTERVAL HISTORY:
Completed approximately 12 months of intermittent ferrous sulfate (originally prescribed for 3 months but family extended at home). Patient reports feeling well. Energy good. Back at school full-time. Periods regular, now monthly, normal duration. No new symptoms.

ROS: All negative.

VITALS: BP 110/68, HR 76, RR 14, T 98.4 F. Weight 51.2 kg. BMI 23.0.

EXAM: Well-appearing 16-year-old female. HEENT, CV, lungs, abdomen, skin all normal.

LABS (today):
- CBC: WBC 5.6, Hgb 13.4 (normal), MCV 88 (normal), Plt 286 — anemia RESOLVED.
- Ferritin 38 (normal).

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. Iron-deficiency anemia — RESOLVED. Discontinue ferrous sulfate. Continue multivitamin.
2. Continue immunization catch-up — HPV #3 given today.
3. RTC annually or PRN.

Visit time: 25 min.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Linda Wong, MD
Date: 11/12/2019
